original_reasoning: Arabic emaciation/change can plausibly connect to Greek 'regimens, ways of life' through diet causing bodily alteration. This is a credible action-to-result chain.
Both contain bghd/baga element (Iranian 'god-given'). Baghdad=Bagh+dad, Bagadates=same root. Plausible loan.
Arabic ghafara = forgive/cover; Greek Gaphara = place in Africa (Tunisia). Shared Semitic/Punic substrate plausible for place name.

Arabic khiba'/khaba = made/set up a tent, hiding place. Greek chabos (Hesychius) = muzzle, kemos. Both denote covering/concealing structures. Phonetic resemblance khib/chab is direct. Strong combined link.
Arabic includes assaying or evaluating dinars, and Greek means equal-sided. The bridge is measurement, balance, and exact proportion, which makes the match plausible but not direct.
مكث=to linger/delay/tarry; μοχθέω=to be weary with toil. Related via notion of prolonged effort/stay
Arabic swollen belly suggests bulging projection, which can conceptually align with a promontory jutting outward.
Arabic مخاضنة includes obscene exchange, and Greek buttocks belongs to the same coarse bodily domain.
